prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ARIMA', seasonality_champion='DWT_rf', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.83) and high seasonality variance (0.54), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ARIMA excels at trend tracking while DWT_rf dominates seasonal capture. The mixed tier structure with strong early/late horizon specialists supports DBA combination approach to leverage complementary strengths across different forecast horizons.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ARIMA",
  "seasonality_champion": "DWT_rf",
  "overall_champion": "THETA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ARIMA", "late": "FT_svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["THETA", "ETS", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "ARIMA", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["DWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "rf", "ONLY_FT_rf", "svr", "FT_svr", "FT_rf", "DWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.8285,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.83) and high seasonality variance (0.54), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ARIMA excels at trend tracking while DWT_rf dominates seasonal capture. The mixed tier structure with strong early/late horizon specialists supports DBA combination approach to leverage complementary strengths across different forecast horizons."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3"],
  "params_overrides": {"inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.35}, "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=6 indicates strong per-horizon specialization, favoring best_per_horizon_by_validation and topk_mean_per_horizon_k3. relative_spread_mean=0.37 (≥0.25) signals high disagreement, justifying dba_combination for phase-shift robustness. n_windows=3 is very few, so shrinkage=0.35 with top_k=3 prevents overfitting. RMSE spread ratio=0.83 confirms high model dispersion, supporting rmse_focus preset."
}
raw.skeptic: {"add_names": [], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current set has 4 candidates with good diversity: 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No type has ≥4 candidates. All candidates are safe from leakage (n_windows=3, using anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection). Score margin between top2 is small (0.0045) indicating competitive set. Universe leaderboards show no candidate significantly outperforms current set across all metrics. No changes needed.", "changes": [], "when_good": ""}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["ridge_stacking_l210_topk5", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "n_windows=3 (low) requires stronger regularization per recommended_knobs. relative_spread_mean=0.368≥0.25 favors DBA/trimmed_mean approaches. n_unique_winners=6≥3 supports best_per_horizon_by_validation (already present). Current set lacks ridge_stacking which excels at POCID (universe leaderboard shows 75.76 vs current ~72.73). Adding topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 provides diversity with k=5 (sqrt(26)≈5.1) for outlier reduction. No removals needed - all current candidates are non-baseline weighted/selection methods.", "changes": ["added ridge_stacking_l210_topk5 for high POCID performance", "added topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 for k=sqrt(n_models) diversity"], "when_good": ""}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.83) and high seasonality variance (0.54), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ARIMA excels at trend tracking while DWT_rf dominates seasonal capture. The mixed tier structure with strong early/late horizon specialists supports DBA combination approach to leverage complementary strengths across different forecast horizons.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=6 indicates strong per-horizon specialization, favoring best_per_horizon_by_validation and topk_mean_per_horizon_k3. relative_spread_mean=0.37 (≥0.25) signals high disagreement, justifying dba_combination for phase-shift robustness. n_windows=3 is very few, so shrinkage=0.35 with top_k=3 prevents overfitting. RMSE spread ratio=0.83 confirms high model dispersion, supporting rmse_focus preset.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 4 candidates with good diversity: 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No type has ≥4 candidates. All candidates are safe from leakage (n_windows=3, using anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection). Score margin between top2 is small (0.0045) indicating competitive set. Universe leaderboards show no candidate significantly outperforms current set across all metrics. No changes needed.

statistician.rationale: n_windows=3 (low) requires stronger regularization per recommended_knobs. relative_spread_mean=0.368≥0.25 favors DBA/trimmed_mean approaches. n_unique_winners=6≥3 supports best_per_horizon_by_validation (already present). Current set lacks ridge_stacking which excels at POCID (universe leaderboard shows 75.76 vs current ~72.73). Adding topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 provides diversity with k=5 (sqrt(26)≈5.1) for outlier reduction. No removals needed - all current candidates are non-baseline weighted/selection methods.
changes: added ridge_stacking_l210_topk5 for high POCID performance
